note: EP008 pricing INPUTS. Rates pulled from PRIMARY vendor pages on 2026-06-14 (URLs below). Plan floors + Supabase compute ladder are published and exact. Pinecone per-unit $/RU,$/WU,$/GB-mo and Qdrant Standard $/mo are NOT published on the pricing pages (calculator-gated) -> left null; confirm in each vendor calculator at record time and screenshot for b-roll. No stale dollars in a render.
verified_on: 2026-06-14 (assistant pull from primary pages; the author to re-glance + screenshot at record)

pinecone_serverless.starter_free_tier: Free: 2GB storage, 2M write units/mo, 1M read units/mo, AWS us-east-1 only, up to 5 indexes
pinecone_serverless.unit_model: query = 1 RU per 1GB namespace (min 0.25 RU); upsert = 1 WU per 1KB (min 5 WU); storage billed per-GB-month. Index size = records x (ID bytes + metadata bytes + dims x 4 bytes).
pinecone_serverless.per_unit_note: Per-unit $ rates are NOT on the pricing page -> use https://www.pinecone.io/pricing/estimate/ . Story: at ~1M x 384-dim (~1.5GB) you sit under the 2GB Starter FREE tier, or pay the $50 Standard floor; per-unit rates only bite at 10M+ vectors.

qdrant_cloud.standard_pricing_model: usage-based: vCPU + RAM + storage billed hourly; NO flat $/mo published
qdrant_cloud.note: qdrant.tech/pricing shows no flat number; must size in calculator. 3rd-party blogs estimate ~$25-45/mo for ~1M vectors -- UNVERIFIED, do NOT render as fact.

supabase_pgvector.pgvector_charge: INCLUDED free on all plans (no separate vector charge)
supabase_pgvector.cost_model: monthly = $25 Pro base + compute tier sized to hold the HNSW index in RAM (minus $10 included credit) + disk over 8GB. Vector workloads are RAM-bound, so the compute tier is the cost driver.

self_hosted_basis.note: reuse EP002/EP007 electricity basis (run_benchmark.py helpers) + RAM-bound sizing: vectors_gb = n * dim * 4 / 1e9; HNSW overhead ~1.5x
